decisions: Preserve stored chord strings and alphaTex generation; limit the change to rendered chord-name typography.
Use a Jazz shorthand convention: baseline quality symbols and extension numbers, with only alterations and modifiers raised.
risks: alphaTab regenerates SVG chord-name text asynchronously, so the visual transformation must be idempotent.
Compact suffix rendering must preserve slash-bass legibility and avoid touching unrelated SVG text.

refinement_notes.note: User corrected the notation convention: use Jazz shorthand symbols such as triangle-seven and minus-seven on the baseline; raise only alterations and modifiers such as flat-nine and sharp-five; ground the mapping in SMuFL and official scoring-application references before implementation.

User rejected the first browser result as hard to read and explicitly requested three to five iterative improvements. Reopen visual design: judge perceptual chord separation rather than bounding-box overlap alone; tune modifier size and rise; preserve Jazz shorthand; browser-check each iteration at 390px with representative dense rows and sharp alterations.

history.event: Planner completed plan.json; Supervisor routed the run to Design Agent.
Design Agent completed browser-backed design.json with the music-visual-instrument profile; Supervisor routed the run to Builder.

Refinement requested for planner: User corrected the notation convention: use Jazz shorthand symbols such as triangle-seven and minus-seven on the baseline; raise only alterations and modifiers such as flat-nine and sharp-five; ground the mapping in SMuFL and official scoring-application references before implementation.
Planner revised plan.json after notation-reference research; Supervisor routed the refinement to Design Agent.
Design Agent completed the reference-backed Jazz shorthand mapping; Supervisor routed the run to Builder.
Builder implemented the approved SVG chord-symbol mapping without changing source chord data; Supervisor routed the run to Verifier.
Verifier passed formatting, lint, typecheck, build, mobile notation, overlap, sharp-alteration, redundant-label, and available-width checks; Supervisor routed the run to Design Critic.
Design Critic approved the reference-backed notation hierarchy and browser evidence; Supervisor routed the run to Code Critic.
Code Critic approved the scoped, idempotent SVG transformation; Supervisor marked the run done.

Refinement requested for design-agent: User rejected the first browser result as hard to read and explicitly requested three to five iterative improvements. Reopen visual design: judge perceptual chord separation rather than bounding-box overlap alone; tune modifier size and rise; preserve Jazz shorthand; browser-check each iteration at 390px with representative dense rows and sharp alterations.
Design Agent rejected global center alignment after browser evidence and approved responsive three-measure mobile systems plus explicit modifier sizing and rise; Supervisor routed the run to Builder.
Builder completed four browser-backed iterations and selected three mobile measures per row with 80% modifiers at 38% rise; Supervisor routed the run to Verifier.

Verifier passed formatting, lint, typecheck, build, browser integrity, four-iteration evidence, mobile fit, notation semantics, and desktop regression checks; Supervisor routed the run to Design Critic.
Design Critic approved the perceptual grouping, responsive density, modifier hierarchy, and mobile/desktop evidence; Supervisor routed the run to Code Critic.
Code Critic approved the scoped responsive settings and SVG modifier changes after React/Next.js review; Supervisor marked the refinement done.
